servers.name: Buildkite
servers.description: Manage [Buildkite](https://buildkite.com) pipelines and builds.

tools.name: access_token
tools.description: Get information about the current API access token including its scopes and UUID

tools.name: create_build
tools.description: Trigger a new build on a Buildkite pipeline for a specific commit and branch, with optional environment variables, metadata, and author information

tools.name: create_cluster
tools.description: Create a new cluster in an organization

tools.name: create_cluster_queue
tools.description: Create a new queue in a cluster

tools.name: create_pipeline
tools.description: Set up a new CI/CD pipeline in Buildkite with YAML configuration, repository connection, and cluster assignment

tools.name: current_user
tools.description: Get details about the user account that owns the API token, including name, email, avatar, and account creation date

tools.name: get_artifact
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific artifact including its metadata, file size, SHA-1 hash, and download URL

tools.name: get_build
tools.description: Get build information including job IDs, names, and states. Use job_state to filter (e.g. 'failed,broken'). Returns enough detail to identify which jobs to investigate with log and artifact tools

tools.name: get_build_test_engine_runs
tools.description: Get test engine runs data for a specific build in Buildkite. This can be used to look up Test Runs.

tools.name: get_cluster
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific cluster including its name, description, default queue, and configuration

tools.name: get_cluster_queue
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific queue including its key, description, dispatch status, and hosted agent configuration

tools.name: get_failed_executions
tools.description: Get failed test executions for a specific test run in Buildkite Test Engine. Optionally get the expanded failure details such as full error messages and stack traces.

tools.name: get_pipeline
tools.description: Get detailed information about a specific pipeline including its configuration, steps, environment variables, and build statistics

tools.name: get_test
tools.description: Get a specific test in Buildkite Test Engine. This provides additional metadata for failed test executions

tools.name: get_test_run
tools.description: Get a specific test run in Buildkite Test Engine

tools.name: list_annotations
tools.description: List all annotations for a build, including their context, style (success/info/warning/error), rendered HTML content, and creation timestamps

tools.name: list_artifacts_for_build
tools.description: List all artifacts for a build across all jobs, including file details, paths, sizes, MIME types, and download URLs

tools.name: list_artifacts_for_job
tools.description: List all artifacts for an individual job, including file details, paths, sizes, MIME types, and download URLs

tools.name: list_builds
tools.description: List builds for a pipeline or across all pipelines in an organization. When pipeline_slug is omitted, lists builds across all pipelines in the organization

tools.name: list_cluster_queues
tools.description: List all queues in a cluster with their keys, descriptions, dispatch status, and agent configuration

tools.name: list_clusters
tools.description: List all clusters in an organization with their names, descriptions, default queues, and creation details

tools.name: list_pipelines
tools.description: List all pipelines in an organization with their basic details, build counts, and current status

tools.name: list_test_runs
tools.description: List all test runs for a test suite in Buildkite Test Engine

tools.name: pause_cluster_queue_dispatch
tools.description: Pause dispatch on a cluster queue, preventing new jobs from being dispatched to agents

tools.name: read_logs
tools.description: Read log entries from the file, optionally starting from a specific row number. ALWAYS use 'limit' parameter to avoid excessive tokens. For recent failures, use 'tail_logs' instead. Recommended limits: investigation (100-500), exploration (use seek + small limits). The json format: {ts: timestamp_ms, c: content, rn: row_number}.

tools.name: resume_cluster_queue_dispatch
tools.description: Resume dispatch on a paused cluster queue, allowing jobs to be dispatched to agents again

tools.name: search_logs
tools.description: Search log entries using regex patterns with optional context lines. For recent failures, try 'tail_logs' first, then use search_logs with patterns like 'error|failed|exception' and limit: 10-20. The json format: {ts: timestamp_ms, c: content, rn: row_number}.

tools.name: tail_logs
tools.description: Show the last N entries from the log file. RECOMMENDED for failure diagnosis - most build failures appear in the final log entries. More token-efficient than read_logs for recent issues. The json format: {ts: timestamp_ms, c: content, rn: row_number}.

tools.name: unblock_job
tools.description: Unblock a blocked job in a Buildkite build to allow it to continue execution

tools.name: update_cluster
tools.description: Update an existing cluster's name, description, emoji, color, or default queue

tools.name: update_cluster_queue
tools.description: Update an existing cluster queue's description or retry agent affinity

tools.name: update_pipeline
tools.description: Modify an existing Buildkite pipeline's configuration, repository, settings, or metadata

tools.name: user_token_organization
tools.description: Get the organization associated with the user token used for this request

servers.name: ClickHouse
servers.description: Query your [ClickHouse](https://clickhouse.com/) database server.

tools.name: get_clickpipe
tools.description: Returns the specified ClickPipe.

tools.name: get_organizations
tools.description: Retrieves all accessible ClickHouse Cloud organizations.

tools.name: run_select_query
tools.description: Executes a SELECT query against a ClickHouse service. Runs a read-only SELECT query on the specified ClickHouse service via the Query API endpoint. This function provides direct access to database content through SQL queries while ensuring that only read operations are permitted.

tools.name: list_databases
tools.description: Retrieves all databases available in a ClickHouse service.

tools.name: list_tables
tools.description: Retrieves a comprehensive list of tables in a ClickHouse database, including columns.

tools.name: get_services_list
tools.description: Retrieves all services in a ClickHouse Cloud organization.

tools.name: get_organization_details
tools.description: Returns details of a single organization. The auth key must belong to the organization.

tools.name: get_service_details
tools.description: Returns a service that belongs to the organization.

tools.name: list_clickpipes
tools.description: Retrieves all ClickPipes configured for a specific service.

tools.name: list_service_backups
tools.description: Returns a list of all backups for the service. The most recent backups comes first in the list.

tools.name: get_organization_cost
tools.description: Retrieves billing and usage cost data for a ClickHouse Cloud organization. Returns a grand total and a list of daily, per-entity organization usage cost records for the organization in the queried time period (maximum 31 days).

tools.name: get_service_backup_details
tools.description: Returns a single backup info.

tools.name: get_service_backup_configuration
tools.description: Returns the service backup configuration.

tools.name: CONTENTFUL_GET_SPACE
tools.description: Retrieves details of a specific space by its ID. This action is used to get information about a single space in Contentful.

tools.name: CONTENTFUL_LIST_SPACES
tools.description: Lists all spaces that the authenticated user has access to in Contentful. This is a fundamental operation needed before performing any other content management actions since most operations require a space ID.

tools.name: CONTENTFUL_UPDATE_SPACE
tools.description: Updates the name of a specific space. Notes: - The version number is required to ensure you're updating the latest version of the space. - You can get the current version from the sys.version field when fetching space details. - Only the name of the space can be updated.

servers.name: Cortex
servers.description: A dynamic, governed memory layer for Markdown notes that serves knowledge to AI clients and humans through a secure MCP server, with scoped access, git-audited changes, and optional LLM-powered semantic search.

tools.name: listAllEntities
tools.description: List and filter catalog entities with support for pagination, search, and various filters including groups, types, owners, and git repositories. If the client is trying to fetch data for teams, use 'type': 'team' in these APIs.

tools.name: listEntityDescriptors
tools.description: Cortex Catalog API - Access and manage your service catalog, teams, domains, and resources

tools.name: listDependenciesForEntity
tools.description: List all dependencies for an entity including both incoming (who depends on this service) and outgoing (what this service depends on) relationships. Essential for understanding service interactions, planning changes, and assessing blast radius

tools.name: getDependency
tools.description: Get specific dependency details between two entities including method, path, and metadata. Useful for understanding the nature of the relationship, API contracts, and communication patterns between services

tools.name: getEntityDetails
tools.description: Retrieve comprehensive details about a specific entity including its metadata, ownership, hierarchies, and relationships. This is the primary method for getting complete information about services, teams, or domains.

tools.name: getCustomDataForEntity
tools.description: List all custom data key-value pairs associated with an entity. Retrieve metadata, configuration settings, and custom attributes stored for services, resources, or domains. Supports pagination for entities with large amounts of custom data

tools.name: getCustomDataForEntityByKey
tools.description: Retrieve a specific custom data value by key for an entity. Efficiently access individual metadata attributes, configuration values, or custom properties without fetching all custom data

tools.name: listCustomEventsForEntity
tools.description: List custom events for an entity with optional filtering by type and time range. Supports pagination and filtering by event type, start time, and end time to retrieve historical event data

tools.name: getCustomEventForEntityByUuid
tools.description: Retrieve a specific custom event by its UUID. Returns event details including title, description, timestamp, type, and any custom data associated with the event

tools.name: getDeploysForEntity
tools.description: List all deployments for a specific catalog entity. Returns deployment history including timestamps, environments, SHAs, and deployment types in paginated format

tools.name: getCurrentOncallForEntity
tools.description: Retrieve the current on-call personnel for an entity in real-time. Shows who is actively responsible for incident response, including primary and secondary on-call, contact information, and rotation schedules

tools.name: getEntityDescriptor
tools.description: Cortex Catalog API - Access and manage your service catalog, teams, domains, and resources

tools.name: listEntityDestinationsForRelationshipType
tools.description: List all destinations for a certain relationship type & entity. Use the listRelationshipTypes tool to find the relevant relationshipTypeTag.

tools.name: listEntitySourcesForRelationshipType
tools.description: List all sources for a certain relationship type & entity. Use the listRelationshipTypes tool to find the relevant relationshipTypeTag.

tools.name: getCustomMetricData
tools.description: Retrieve custom metric data points for an entity. Returns paginated time-series data for a specific custom metric, with optional filtering by date range to analyze trends and patterns

tools.name: queryPointInTimeMetrics
tools.description:
Execute point-in-time queries for one or more engineering metrics.

        Returns current metric values for specified time periods, with support for batch queries
        and optional period-over-period comparisons. Time range (startTime/endTime) cannot exceed 6 months (180 days).

        PREREQUISITES - Follow this workflow:
        1. Discover all available metrics ONCE: Call listMetricDefinitions (view='basic') - cache this response
        2. Get metric query metadata ONCE per metric: Cal

tools.name: listMetricDefinitions
tools.description:
List all available engineering metric definitions.

        USAGE - Call this endpoint BEFORE querying metrics (queryPointInTimeMetrics):
        1. Once at start: Call with view='basic' to discover all available metrics - cache this response
        2. Once per metric: Call with view='full' and key=METRIC_KEY to get detailed metadata - cache each response
        3. Use cached metadata to construct valid point-in-time queries

        Cache responses in your context. Only refresh if no longer i

tools.name: listInitiatives
tools.description: List all initiatives in the organization with optional filters for draft and expired initiatives. View active improvement programs, strategic projects, and their current status to understand organizational priorities and track progress

tools.name: getInitiative
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific initiative including its goals, timeline, affected entities, scorecard targets, and current progress. Essential for understanding initiative scope and tracking achievement of objectives

tools.name: getMyWorkspace
tools.description:
TOOL for retrieving current user's owned resources and work items across the Cortex workspace.

        FLEXIBLE REQUEST STRUCTURE:
        The request accepts an object with optional fields for each resource type:
        - myEntitiesRequest: Fetch entities (services, resources, domains) owned by the user
        - myTeamsRequest: Fetch teams the user belongs to
        - myScorecardsRequest: Fetch scorecards associated with user's entities
        - myOpenPRsRequest: Fetch user's open pull req

tools.name: listRelationshipTypes
tools.description: List all available relationship types with pagination. View relationship type configurations to understand what kinds of relationships can be created between entities like services, resources, domains, and teams

tools.name: getRelationshipTypeDetails
tools.description: Get complete details of a specific relationship type including its configuration, rules, source/destination filters, and inheritance settings. Essential for understanding how entities can be connected and what validation rules apply

tools.name: listEntityRelationships
tools.description: List all entity relationships/full graph for a specific relationship type across the entire organization. Returns paginated results showing all source-destination pairs, useful for understanding the complete relationship graph and finding all connections of a particular type

tools.name: listScorecards
tools.description: List all scorecards in the organization with optional filtering. View scorecard configurations to understand quality standards, compliance requirements, and maturity models. Supports filtering by groups, entities, and teams to find relevant scorecards

tools.name: getScorecard
tools.description: Get complete details of a scorecard including its configuration, rules, levels, weights, exemption settings, and evaluation criteria. Essential for understanding how services are evaluated and what standards they must meet

tools.name: getScorecardNextStepsForEntity
tools.description: Get actionable next steps for an entity to improve its scorecard performance. Shows which rules need to be satisfied to reach the next maturity level, helping teams prioritize improvements and track progress toward compliance goals

tools.name: listScorecardScores
tools.description: Retrieve scores for all entities evaluated by a specific scorecard. Returns paginated results showing how each service, resource, or domain performs against the scorecard's rules, including individual rule scores and overall scorecard scores

tools.name: getTeamDetails
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific team by its tag or ID. Returns complete team data including members, slack channels, metadata, and whether it's backed by an identity provider group

tools.name: query_docs
tools.description:
Query the Cortex knowledge base for answers.

Args:
    query: The question to ask Cortex docs
    
Returns:
    Response from Cortex including answer and metadata

tools.name: get_more_tools
tools.description: Check for additional tools whenever your task might benefit from specialized capabilities - even if existing tools could work as a fallback.

servers.name: Excel
servers.description: Excel manipulation including data reading/writing, worksheet management, formatting, charts, and pivot table

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_CHART
tools.description: Add a chart to a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_SHAREPOINT_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Add a new worksheet to a SharePoint Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_TABLE
tools.description: Create a new table in a worksheet using the Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_TABLE_COLUMN
tools.description: Add a column to a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_TABLE_ROW
tools.description: Add a row to a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_WORKBOOK_PERMISSION
tools.description: Tool to grant access to a workbook via invite. Use when you need to share a specific workbook file with designated recipients and roles.

tools.name: EXCEL_ADD_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Add a new worksheet to an Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_APPLY_TABLE_FILTER
tools.description: Apply a filter to a table column using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_APPLY_TABLE_SORT
tools.description: Apply a sort to a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_CLEAR_RANGE
tools.description: Tool to clear values, formats, or contents in a specified worksheet range. Use when you need to reset cells before adding new data.

tools.name: EXCEL_CLEAR_TABLE_FILTER
tools.description: Clear a filter from a table column using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_CLOSE_SESSION
tools.description: Tool to close an existing Excel workbook session. Use when you need to explicitly end a persistent session to release workbook locks.

tools.name: EXCEL_CONVERT_TABLE_TO_RANGE
tools.description: Convert a table to a range using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_CREATE_WORKBOOK
tools.description: Tool to create a new Excel workbook file at a specified drive path. Generates a new .xlsx file with specified worksheets and data, then uploads it to OneDrive.

tools.name: EXCEL_DELETE_TABLE_COLUMN
tools.description: Delete a column from a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_DELETE_TABLE_ROW
tools.description: Delete a row from a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_DELETE_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Tool to delete a worksheet from the workbook. Use when cleaning up unused or temporary sheets after verifying no dependencies exist. Example: "Delete 'Sheet2' after review."

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_CHART_AXIS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific axis from a chart. Use when you need properties like min, max, interval, and formatting of the chart axis.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_CHART_DATA_LABELS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the data labels object of a chart. Use when you need to inspect label settings like position, separator, and visibility flags after creating or updating a chart.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_CHART_LEGEND
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the legend object of a chart. Use after creating or updating a chart when you need to inspect legend visibility and formatting.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_RANGE
tools.description: Get a range from a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_SESSION
tools.description: Create a session for an Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_SHAREPOINT_RANGE
tools.description: Get a range from a worksheet in SharePoint using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_SHAREPOINT_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Get a worksheet by name or ID from a SharePoint Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_TABLE_COLUMN
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific column from a workbook table. Use when you need to fetch column properties and data by its ID or name.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_WORKBOOK
tools.description: Tool to retrieve the properties and relationships of a workbook. Use when you need to inspect comments, names, tables, or worksheets.

tools.name: EXCEL_GET_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Get a worksheet by name or ID from an Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_INSERT_RANGE
tools.description: Tool to insert a new cell range into a worksheet, shifting existing cells down or right. Use when you need to create space for new content without overwriting.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_CHARTS
tools.description: List charts in a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_CHART_SERIES
tools.description: Tool to list all data series in a chart. Use when you need to enumerate chart series for further analysis.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_COMMENTS
tools.description: Tool to list comments in an Excel workbook. Use when you need to retrieve all workbook comments via Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_FILES
tools.description: List files and folders in a drive root or specified path.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_NAMED_ITEMS
tools.description: List named items in a workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_SHAREPOINT_TABLES
tools.description: List tables in a SharePoint worksheet using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_SHAREPOINT_WORKSHEETS
tools.description: List worksheets in an Excel workbook stored in SharePoint using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_TABLE_COLUMNS
tools.description: List columns in a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_TABLE_ROWS
tools.description: List rows in a table using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_TABLES
tools.description: List tables in a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API. This action retrieves information about all tables present in a specified worksheet of an Excel file. It requires the file ID and worksheet name or ID, and can optionally use a session ID for workbook operations.

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_WORKBOOK_PERMISSIONS
tools.description: Tool to list permissions set on the workbook file. Use when you need to see which users or links have access to a specific Excel file by supplying its drive and item IDs. Example: "List permissions for workbook with drive_id 'b!abc123' and item_id '0123456789abcdef'."

tools.name: EXCEL_LIST_WORKSHEETS
tools.description: List worksheets in an Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_MERGE_CELLS
tools.description: Merge cells in a worksheet range using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_PROTECT_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Tool to protect a worksheet using optional protection options. Use when you need to prevent editing certain parts of a sheet before sharing. Example: "Protect 'Sheet1' to lock formatting and sorting."

tools.name: EXCEL_SORT_RANGE
tools.description: Sort a range in a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_CHART
tools.description: Update a chart in a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_CHART_LEGEND
tools.description: Tool to update formatting or position of a chart legend. Use when adjusting legend settings after confirming chart and worksheet exist.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_RANGE
tools.description: Update a range in a worksheet using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_SHAREPOINT_RANGE
tools.description: Update a range in a SharePoint worksheet using Microsoft Graph Sites API.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_TABLE
tools.description: Update a table in a workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

tools.name: EXCEL_UPDATE_WORKSHEET
tools.description: Update worksheet properties (name, position) in an Excel workbook using Microsoft Graph API.

servers.name: InstantDB
servers.description: Create, manage, and update applications on InstantDB, the modern Firebase.

tools.name: learn
tools.description: If you don't have any context provided about InstantDB, use this tool to learn about it!

tools.name: get-schema
tools.description: Fetch schema for an app by its ID!

tools.name: get-perms
tools.description: Fetch permissions for an app by its ID

tools.name: push-schema
tools.description:
Push local schema changes for an app to the server. Do this after updating your local 'instant.schema.ts' file.
    If you don't have an instant.schema.ts file yet, use the get-schema tool to learn how to get this file.

tools.name: push-perms
tools.description:
Push local permissions changes for an app to the server. Do this after updating your local instant.perms.ts file.
    If you don't have an instant.perms.ts file yet, use the get-perms tool to learn how to get this file.

tools.name: query
tools.description:
Execute an InstaQL query against an app. Returns the query results as JSON.

    Example query to fetch all goals and their todos:
    {"goals": {"todos": {}}}

    Example query with a where clause:
    {"goals": {"$": {"where": {"status": "active"}}, "todos": {}}}

    If you're unsure how to write queries, refer to the documentation:
    https://instantdb.com/docs/instaql

tools.name: transact
tools.description:
Execute a transaction against an app. Useful for creating, updating, or deleting data.

    Steps use the internal transaction format:
    - Create/update: ["update", "namespace", "entity-id", {"attr": "value"}]
    - Link: ["link", "namespace", "entity-id", {"linkAttr": "target-id"}]
    - Unlink: ["unlink", "namespace", "entity-id", {"linkAttr": "target-id"}]
    - Delete: ["delete", "namespace", "entity-id"]

    Example steps to create a todo:
    [["update", "todos", "a-uuid", {"title": "Ge

servers.name: Mapbox
servers.description: Unlock geospatial intelligence through Mapbox APIs like geocoding, POI search, directions, isochrones and more.

tools.name: place_details_tool
tools.description: Retrieve detailed information about a specific place using its Mapbox ID. Use after search_and_geocode_tool, category_search_tool, or reverse_geocode_tool to get additional details such as photos, opening hours, ratings, phone numbers, and website URLs. Requires the mapbox_id field from a previous search result.

tools.name: simplify_tool
tools.description: Simplify a LineString or Polygon by reducing the number of vertices while preserving the general shape. Uses the Douglas-Peucker algorithm. Useful for reducing file sizes and improving rendering performance. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: bbox_tool
tools.description: Calculate the bounding box (extent) of any geometry. Returns the minimum and maximum longitude and latitude that encompass the geometry. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: centroid_tool
tools.description: Calculate the geometric center (centroid) of a polygon or multipolygon. The centroid is the arithmetic mean position of all points in the shape. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: midpoint_tool
tools.description: Calculate the geographic midpoint between two coordinates. Returns the point that is halfway between the two input points along the great circle path. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: bearing_tool
tools.description: Calculate the bearing (compass direction) from one point to another. Returns bearing in degrees where 0° is North, 90° is East, 180° is South, and 270° is West. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: area_tool
tools.description: Calculate the area of a polygon or multipolygon. Supports various units including square meters, kilometers, acres, and hectares. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: buffer_tool
tools.description: Create a buffer zone (polygon) around a point, line, or polygon at a specified distance. Useful for proximity analysis, service areas, or creating zones of influence. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: point_in_polygon_tool
tools.description: Test if a geographic point is inside a polygon. Useful for determining if a location is within a boundary, service area, or region. Handles polygons with holes. Works offline without API calls.

tools.name: distance_tool
tools.description: Calculate the distance between two geographic coordinates. Supports various units including kilometers, miles, meters, feet, and nautical miles. Uses the Haversine formula for accurate great-circle distance calculations. This tool works offline without requiring API calls.

tools.name: version_tool
tools.description: Get the current version information of the MCP server

tools.name: category_search_tool
tools.description: Return all places that match a category (industry, amenity, or NAICS‑style code). Use when the user asks for a type of place, plural or generic terms like 'museums', 'coffee shops', 'electric‑vehicle chargers', or when the query includes is‑a phrases such as 'any', 'all', 'nearby'. Do not use when a unique name or brand is provided. Supports both JSON and text output formats.

tools.name: directions_tool
tools.description: Fetches directions from Mapbox API based on provided coordinates and direction method. For route planning and distance calculations, use geometries="none" to get compact responses. Only request full geometry (geometries="geojson") when you need to visualize the route on a map or provide detailed turn-by-turn navigation instructions.

tools.name: isochrone_tool
tools.description:
Computes areas that are reachable within a specified amount of time from a location, and returns the reachable regions as contours of Polygons or LineStrings in GeoJSON format that you can display on a map.
  Common use cases:
    - Show a user how far they can travel in X minutes from their current location
    - Determine whether a destination is within a certain travel time threshold
    - Compare travel ranges for different modes of transportation'

tools.name: map_matching_tool
tools.description: Snap GPS traces to roads using Mapbox Map Matching API. Takes noisy/inaccurate coordinate sequences (2-100 points) and returns clean routes aligned with actual roads, bike paths, or walkways. Useful for analyzing recorded trips, cleaning fleet tracking data, or processing fitness activity traces. Returns confidence scores, matched geometry, and optional traffic/speed annotations.

tools.name: matrix_tool
tools.description: Calculates travel times and distances between multiple points using Mapbox Matrix API.

tools.name: optimization_tool
tools.description: Find the optimal (shortest by travel time) route through a set of 2-12 coordinates. Solves the Traveling Salesman Problem to determine the best visiting order. Supports options for starting point, ending point, and whether to return to start.

tools.name: reverse_geocode_tool
tools.description: Find addresses, cities, towns, neighborhoods, postcodes, districts, regions, and countries around a specified geographic coordinate pair. Converts geographic coordinates (longitude, latitude) into human-readable addresses or place names. Use limit=1 for best results. This tool cannot reverse geocode businesses, landmarks, historic sites, and other points of interest that are not of the types mentioned. Supports both JSON and text output formats.

tools.name: static_map_image_tool
tools.description: Generates a static map image from Mapbox Static Images API. Supports center coordinates, zoom level (0-22), image size (up to 1280x1280), various Mapbox styles, and overlays (markers, paths, GeoJSON). Returns PNG for vector styles, JPEG for raster-only styles.

tools.name: search_and_geocode_tool
tools.description: Search for POIs, brands, chains, geocode cities, towns, addresses. Do not use for generic place types such as 'museums', 'coffee shops', 'tacos', etc, because category_search_tool is better for that. Setting a proximity point is strongly encouraged for more local results.

tools.name: resource_reader_tool
tools.description: Reads an MCP resource by URI. This tool is a fallback for clients that do not support the native MCP resource API. Supports reading category lists and other Mapbox resources. Use "mapbox://categories" for default categories or "mapbox://categories/{language}" for localized versions (e.g., "mapbox://categories/ja" for Japanese).

tools.name: category_list_tool
tools.description: [DEPRECATED: Use resource_reader_tool with "mapbox://categories" URI instead] Tool for retrieving the list of supported categories from Mapbox Search API. This tool is kept for backward compatibility with clients that do not support MCP resources. Use this when another function requires a list of categories. Returns all available category IDs by default. Only use pagination (limit/offset) if token usage optimization is required. If using pagination, make multiple calls to retrieve ALL categories

servers.name: Miro
servers.description: Miro MCP server, exposing all functionalities available in official Miro SDK.

tools.name: MIRO_CREATE_APP_CARD_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to add an app card item to a board. Use when you need to push a rich preview card with custom fields into a Miro board (e.g., after assembling card data).

tools.name: MIRO_CREATE_BOARD
tools.description: Tool to create a new board. Use when you need to set up a board with a specific name, description, and policies. Example: "Create a new board named Project Plan".

tools.name: MIRO_DELETE_APP_CARD_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to delete an app card item from a board. Use when you need to remove an app card item created by your app after it is no longer needed.

tools.name: MIRO_DELETE_DOCUMENT_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to delete a document item from a board. Use when you need to remove a document item (e.g., PDF or image) that is no longer relevant. Example: "Delete the document item with ID 'item456' from board 'board123'."

tools.name: MIRO_DELETE_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to delete a specific item from a board. Use when you need to remove an item (e.g., shape, sticky note) after confirming its board and item IDs.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_APP_CARD_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific app card item by its ID. Use when you need the details of an existing app card item.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_BOARD
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details of a specific board. Use when you have a board ID and need to fetch its metadata.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_BOARD_ITEMS
tools.description: Tool to list items on a Miro board (shapes, stickies, cards, etc.) with pagination. Use when you need to page through board elements for analytics or reporting.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_BOARD_MEMBERS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a list of members for a board. Use when you need to list all users with access to a board after confirming its ID.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_BOARDS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve accessible boards with optional filters. Use when you need to list or search boards by team, project, owner, or keywords.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_CONNECTORS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a list of connectors on a board. Use after confirming the board ID and when you need to page through connector items.

tools.name: MIRO_GET_TAG
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details of a specific tag on a board. Use when you have a board ID and tag ID and need its metadata.

tools.name: MIRO_LIST_ORGANIZATIONS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve list of organizations accessible to the user. Use when you need to view all available organizations.

tools.name: MIRO_UPDATE_APP_CARD_ITEM
tools.description: Tool to update an app card item on a board. Use when you need to modify properties of an existing app card item. Include only fields to change.

tools.name: MIRO_UPDATE_BOARD
tools.description: Tool to update properties of a specific board. Use when you have a board ID and need to modify its name, description, or permissions policy. Use after confirming the board exists.

tools.name: octagon-agent
tools.description: Orchestrates all agents for comprehensive market intelligence analysis. Capabilities: Combines insights from SEC filings, earnings calls, financial metrics, stock data, institutional holdings, private company research, funding analysis, M&A transactions, investor intelligence, and debt analysis to provide holistic market intelligence. Best for: Complex research requiring multiple data sources and comprehensive analysis across public and private markets. Example queries: 'Retrieve year-over-year

tools.name: octagon-deep-research-agent
tools.description: A comprehensive agent that can utilize multiple sources for deep research analysis. Capabilities: Aggregate research across multiple data sources, synthesize information, and provide comprehensive investment research. Best for: Investment research questions requiring up-to-date aggregated information from the web. Example queries: 'Research the financial impact of Apple's privacy changes on digital advertising companies' revenue and margins', 'Analyze the competitive landscape in the cloud compu

tools.name: octagon-prediction-markets-agent
tools.description:
A specialized agent for creating research reports on Kalshi events (Polymarket coming soon). See what's driving prices, compare market vs model probabilities, and find potential mispricings.

      Note: It's recommended to attempt to retrieve a cached report first as new reports may take several minutes to generate.
       Example queries: 'https://kalshi.com/markets/kxbtcy/btc-price-range-eoy/kxbtcy-27jan0100', 'https://kalshi.com/markets/kxnbawest/nba-western-conference-championship/kxnbawest

tools.name: prediction_markets_history
tools.description:
Fetch historical data for a prediction market event by ticker.
Supports pagination (limit, cursor) and time window (captured_from, captured_to) and optionally include analysis columns.

Note: For a typical Kalshi Market URL, the event ticker is the final segment in the URL.
For example, for the URL https://kalshi.com/markets/kxbtcy/btc-price-range-eoy/kxbtcy-27jan0100, the event ticker is kxbtcy-27jan0100.

tools.name: create_invoice
tools.description: Creates PayPal Invoice Link

tools.name: list_transactions
tools.description: Fetches details of your PayPal Transactions

tools.name: create_bulk_invoices
tools.description:
Use this tool to create multiple PayPal invoices in bulk via concurrent processing.
IMPORTANT: Do not call this tool directly from raw user input. First validate each 
invoice object, resolve any errors, and confirm the full payload with the user. 
Only invoke this tool after the user has reviewed and approved the data.
Accepts: array of fully validated PayPal invoice objects.

tools.name: send_bulk_invoices
tools.description:
Sends multiple PayPal invoices in bulk with concurrent processing.

IMPORTANT: This action is irreversible — once sent, invoices are delivered to recipients via email.
Do NOT call this tool without first displaying the list of invoice IDs to the user and receiving
explicit confirmation to proceed. Confirm send options (recipient/invoicer) with the user as well.

NOTE: Default send_to_recipient is true and if an invoice has no primary_recipients (i.e. was created
without a recipient email), PayPa

servers.name: Postman
servers.description: Postman’s remote MCP server connects AI agents, assistants, and chatbots directly to your APIs on Postman.

tools.name: createCollection
tools.description:
Creates a collection using the [Postman Collection v2.1.0 schema format](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

If you do not include the \`workspace\` query parameter, the system creates the collection in the oldest personal Internal workspace you own.

tools.name: createCollectionRequest
tools.description:
Creates a request in a collection. For a complete list of properties, refer to the **Request** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

It is recommended that you pass the \`name\` property in the request body. If you do not, the system uses a null value. As a result, this creates a request with a blank name.

tools.name: createCollectionResponse
tools.description:
Creates a request response in a collection. For a complete list of request body properties, refer to the **Response** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

It is recommended that you pass the \`name\` property in the request body. If you do not, the system uses a null value. As a result, this creates a response with a blank name.

tools.name: createEnvironment
tools.description:
Creates an environment.

**Note:**

- The request body size cannot exceed the maximum allowed size of 30MB.
- If you receive an HTTP \`411 Length Required\` error response, manually pass the \`Content-Length\` header and its value in the request header.
- If you do not include the \`workspace\` query parameter, the system creates the environment in the oldest personal Internal workspace you own.

tools.name: createMock
tools.description:
Creates a mock server in a collection.

- Pass the collection UID (ownerId-collectionId), not the bare collection ID.
- If you only have a \`collectionId\`, resolve the UID first:
  1) Prefer GET \`/collections/{collectionId}\` and read \`uid\`, or
  2) Construct \`{ownerId}-{collectionId}\` using ownerId from GET \`/me\`:
    - For team-owned collections: \`ownerId = me.teamId\`
    - For personal collections: \`ownerId = me.user.id\`
- Use the \`workspace\` query to place the mock in a specifi

tools.name: createSpec
tools.description:
Creates an API specification in Postman's [Spec Hub](https://learning.postman.com/docs/design-apis/specifications/overview/). Specifications can be single or multi-file.

**Note:**
- Postman supports OpenAPI 2.0, OpenAPI 3.0, OpenAPI 3.1, AsyncAPI 2.0, protobuf 2 and 3, and GraphQL specifications.
- If the file path contains a \`/\` (forward slash) character, then a folder is created. For example, if the path is the \`components/schemas.json\` value, then a \`components\` folder is created with

tools.name: createSpecFile
tools.description:
Creates an API specification file.

**Note:**

- If the file path contains a \`/\` (forward slash) character, then a folder is created. For example, if the path is the \`components/schemas.json\` value, then a \`components\` folder is created with the \`schemas.json\` file inside.
- Creating a spec file assigns it the \`DEFAULT\` file type.
- Multi-file specifications can only have one root file.
- Files cannot exceed a maximum of 10 MB in size.

tools.name: createWorkspace
tools.description:
Creates a new [workspace](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/using-workspaces/creating-workspaces/).

**Note:**

- This endpoint returns a 403 \`Forbidden\` response if the user does not have permission to create workspaces. [Admins and Super Admins](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/roles-and-permissions/#team-roles) can configure workspace permissions to restrict users and/or user groups from creating workspaces or require approvals for the crea

tools.name: duplicateCollection
tools.description:
Creates a duplicate of the given collection in another workspace.

Use the GET \`/collection-duplicate-tasks/{taskId}\` endpoint to get the duplication task's current status.

tools.name: generateCollection
tools.description:
Creates a collection from the given API specification.
The specification must already exist or be created before it can be used to generate a collection.
The response contains a polling link to the task status.

tools.name: generateSpecFromCollection
tools.description: Generates an API specification for the given collection. The response contains a polling link to the task status.

tools.name: getAllSpecs
tools.description: Gets all API specifications in a workspace.

tools.name: getAuthenticatedUser
tools.description:
Gets information about the authenticated user.
- This endpoint provides “current user” context (\`user.id\`, \`username\`, \`teamId\`, roles).
- When a user asks for “my …” (e.g., “my workspaces, my information, etc.”), call this first to resolve the user ID.

tools.name: getCollection
tools.description:
Get information about a collection. By default this tool returns the lightweight collection map (metadata + recursive itemRefs).
Use the model parameter to opt in to Postman's full API responses:
- model=minimal — root-level folder/request IDs only
- model=full — full Postman collection payload.

tools.name: getCollections
tools.description: The workspace ID query is required for this endpoint. If not provided, the LLM should ask the user to provide it.

tools.name: getDuplicateCollectionTaskStatus
tools.description: Gets the status of a collection duplication task.

tools.name: getEnabledTools
tools.description: IMPORTANT: Run this tool first when a requested tool is unavailable. Returns information about which tools are enabled in the full and minimal tool sets, helping you identify available alternatives.

tools.name: getEnvironment
tools.description: Gets information about an environment.

tools.name: getEnvironments
tools.description: Gets information about all of your [environments](https://learning.postman.com/docs/sending-requests/managing-environments/).

tools.name: getGeneratedCollectionSpecs
tools.description: Gets the API specification generated for the given collection.

tools.name: getMock
tools.description:
Gets information about a mock server.
- Resource: Mock server entity. Response includes the associated \`collection\` UID and \`mockUrl\`.
- Use the \`collection\` UID to navigate back to the source collection.

tools.name: getMocks
tools.description:
Gets all active mock servers. By default, returns only mock servers you created across all workspaces.

- Always pass either the \`workspace\` or \`teamId\` query to scope results. Prefer \`workspace\` when known.
- If you need team-scoped results, set \`teamId\` from the current user: call GET \`/me\` and use \`me.teamId\`.
- If both \`teamId\` and \`workspace\` are passed, only \`workspace\` is used.

tools.name: getSpec
tools.description: Gets information about an API specification.

tools.name: getSpecCollections
tools.description: Gets all of an API specification's generated collections.

tools.name: getSpecDefinition
tools.description: Gets the complete contents of an API specification's definition.

tools.name: getSpecFile
tools.description: Gets the contents of an API specification's file.

tools.name: getSpecFiles
tools.description: Gets all the files in an API specification.

tools.name: getTaggedEntities
tools.description:
**Requires an Enterprise plan.** Tagging is only available on Postman Enterprise plans. This tool returns a 404 error on Free, Basic, and Professional accounts.

Gets Postman elements (entities) by a given tag. Tags enable you to organize and search workspaces, APIs, and collections that contain shared tags.

tools.name: getWorkspace
tools.description:
Gets information about a workspace.

**Note:**

This endpoint's response contains the \`visibility\` field. [Visibility](https://learning.postman.com/docs/collaborating-in-postman/using-workspaces/managing-workspaces/#changing-workspace-visibility) determines who can access the workspace:
- \`personal\` — Only you can access the workspace.
- \`team\` — All team members can access the workspace.
- \`private\` — Only invited team members can access the workspace ([**Team** and **Enterprise** plans

tools.name: getWorkspaces
tools.description:
Gets all workspaces you have access to.
- For “my …” requests, first call GET \`/me\` and pass \`createdBy={me.user.id}\`.
- This endpoint's response contains the visibility field. Visibility determines who can access the workspace:
  - \`personal\` — Only you can access the workspace.
  - \`team\` — All team members can access the workspace.
  - \`private\` — Only invited team members can access the workspace (Professional and Enterprise).
  - \`public\` — Everyone can access the workspace.
  -

tools.name: publishMock
tools.description: Publishes a mock server. Publishing a mock server sets its **Access Control** configuration setting to public.

tools.name: putCollection
tools.description:
Replaces the contents of a collection using the [Postman Collection v2.1.0 schema format](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html). Include the collection's ID values in the request body. If you do not, the endpoint removes the existing items and creates new items.

- To perform an update asynchronously, use the \`Prefer\` header with the \`respond-async\` value. When performing an async update, this endpoint returns a HTTP \`202 Accepted\` response.
- For a co

tools.name: putEnvironment
tools.description:
Replaces all the contents of an environment with the given information.

**Note:**

- The request body size cannot exceed the maximum allowed size of 30MB.
- If you receive an HTTP \`411 Length Required\` error response, manually pass the \`Content-Length\` header and its value in the request header.

tools.name: runCollection
tools.description: Runs a Postman collection by ID with detailed test results and execution statistics. Supports optional environment for variable substitution. Note: Advanced parameters like custom delays and other runtime options are not yet available.

tools.name: syncCollectionWithSpec
tools.description:
Syncs a collection generated from an API specification. This is an asynchronous endpoint that returns an HTTP \`202 Accepted\` response.

**Note:**

- This endpoint only supports the OpenAPI 3.0 specification type.
- You can only sync collections generated from the given spec ID.

tools.name: syncSpecWithCollection
tools.description:
Syncs an API specification linked to a collection. This is an asynchronous endpoint that returns an HTTP \`202 Accepted\` response.

**Note:**

- This endpoint only supports the OpenAPI 3.0 specification type.
- You can only sync specs generated from the given collection ID.

tools.name: updateCollectionRequest
tools.description:
Updates a request in a collection. For a complete list of properties, refer to the **Request** entry in the [Postman Collection Format documentation](https://schema.postman.com/collection/json/v2.1.0/draft-07/docs/index.html).

**Note:**

- You must pass a collection ID (\`12ece9e1-2abf-4edc-8e34-de66e74114d2\`), not a collection(\`12345678-12ece9e1-2abf-4edc-8e34-de66e74114d2\`), in this endpoint.
- This endpoint does not support changing the folder of a request.
- This endpoint acts like a PAT

tools.name: updateMock
tools.description:
Updates a mock server.
- Resource: Mock server entity associated with a collection UID.
- Use this to change name, environment, privacy, or default server response.

tools.name: updateSpecFile
tools.description:
Updates an API specification's file.

**Note:**

- This endpoint does not accept an empty request body. You must pass one of the accepted values.
- This endpoint does not accept multiple request body properties in a single call. For example, you cannot pass both the \`content\` and \`type\` property at the same time.
- Multi-file specifications can only have one root file.
- When updating a file type to \`ROOT\`, the previous root file is updated to the \`DEFAULT\` file type.
- Files cannot exce

tools.name: updateSpecProperties
tools.description: Updates an API specification's properties, such as its name.

tools.name: updateWorkspace
tools.description:
Updates a workspace.

**Note:**

- This endpoint does not support the following visibility changes:
  - \`private\` to \`public\`, \`public\` to \`private\`, and \`private\` to \`personal\` for **Free** and **Solo** [plans](https://www.postman.com/pricing/).
  - \`public\` to \`personal\` for team users only.
- There are rate limits when publishing public workspaces.
- Public team workspace names must be unique.

servers.name: Square
servers.description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server for square

tools.name: SQUARE_ACCEPT_DISPUTE
tools.description: Tool to accept a dispute and mark it as lost. Use after reviewing all evidence and deciding to accept liability.

tools.name: SQUARE_CANCEL_INVOICE
tools.description: Tool to cancel an invoice. Use when you need to void an existing invoice; ensure the invoice isn't in DRAFT or a terminal state before cancelling.

tools.name: SQUARE_CANCEL_PAYMENT
tools.description: Tool to cancel a payment. Use after confirming the payment is in APPROVED status.

tools.name: SQUARE_CREATE_CARD
tools.description: Tool to create a card on file. Use when you need to securely store a customer's card for future payments. Requires a valid nonce or payment ID.

tools.name: SQUARE_CREATE_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE_FILE
tools.description: Tool to upload a file as dispute evidence. Use when you need to attach supporting documents (PDF, image, etc.) to an existing dispute. Use after creating or retrieving a dispute.

tools.name: SQUARE_CREATE_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE_TEXT
tools.description: Tool to upload text as evidence for a dispute challenge. Endpoint: POST /v2/disputes/{dispute_id}/evidence-text

tools.name: SQUARE_CREATE_INVOICE_ATTACHMENT
tools.description: Tool to upload a file and attach it to a Square invoice. Use when you need to add supplementary documents to an invoice in DRAFT, SCHEDULED, UNPAID, or PARTIALLY_PAID state.

tools.name: SQUARE_DELETE_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE
tools.description: Tool to delete specified evidence from a dispute. Use when you need to remove an evidence item that is no longer relevant. Use after verifying dispute_id and evidence_id.

tools.name: SQUARE_DELETE_INVOICE
tools.description: Tool to delete a Square invoice (only DRAFT invoices can be deleted).

tools.name: SQUARE_DELETE_INVOICE_ATTACHMENT
tools.description: Tool to delete an attachment from a Square invoice. Endpoint: DELETE /v2/invoices/{invoice_id}/attachments/{attachment_id}

tools.name: SQUARE_DELETE_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTION
tools.description: Tool to delete a Square webhook subscription by ID.

tools.name: SQUARE_GET_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific evidence item associated with a given dispute. Endpoint: GET /v2/disputes/{dispute_id}/evidence/{evidence_id}

tools.name: SQUARE_GET_INVOICE
tools.description: Tool to retrieve an invoice by ID. Use when you already have an invoice ID and need its details.

tools.name: SQUARE_LIST_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE
tools.description: Tool to list evidence items associated with a given dispute. Endpoint: GET /v2/disputes/{dispute_id}/evidence

tools.name: SQUARE_LIST_INVOICES
tools.description: Tool to list invoices for a Square location.

tools.name: SQUARE_LIST_WEBHOOK_EVENT_TYPES
tools.description: Tool to list available webhook event types. Use when you need to discover event types before creating or updating a webhook subscription.

tools.name: SQUARE_LIST_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTIONS
tools.description: Tool to list webhook subscriptions. Use when you need to retrieve a page of webhook subscriptions for your application; supports pagination.

tools.name: SQUARE_RETRIEVE_DISPUTE
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a Square dispute by ID. Endpoint: GET /v2/disputes/{dispute_id}

tools.name: SQUARE_RETRIEVE_ORDER
tools.description: Tool to retrieve an order by its ID. Use when you have an order ID and need to fetch its details.

tools.name: SQUARE_RETRIEVE_PAYMENT_LINK
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a payment link by ID. Use when you need to fetch full details of a specific checkout link.

tools.name: SQUARE_RETRIEVE_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTION
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a Square webhook subscription by ID.

tools.name: SQUARE_SUBMIT_DISPUTE_EVIDENCE
tools.description: Tool to submit evidence for a dispute. Use when you have compiled all supporting documentation and want to send it to the cardholder's bank before the due date.

tools.name: SQUARE_TEST_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTION
tools.description: Tool to test a webhook subscription. Use after creating or updating a subscription to verify its endpoint.

tools.name: SQUARE_UPDATE_ORDER
tools.description: Tool to update an order. Use when modifying fields on an existing open order; requires the latest version for optimistic concurrency.

tools.name: SQUARE_UPDATE_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTION
tools.description: Tool to update a Square webhook subscription. Use when you need to modify subscription settings such as name, enabled state, event types, notification URL, or API version of an existing webhook subscription.

tools.name: SQUARE_UPDATE_WEBHOOK_SUBSCRIPTION_SIGNATURE_KEY
tools.description: Tool to rotate the signature key for a webhook subscription. Use when you need to replace the current signature key for security purposes.

3. MCP endpoint: https://square.run.tools

servers.name: Tinybird
servers.description: Interact with Tinybird serverless ClickHouse platform

tools.name: explore_data
tools.description: Explore data in the workspace to answer questions.

tools.name: text_to_sql
tools.description: Convert natural language question to SQL query.

tools.name: list_datasources
tools.description: List all datasources available in the workspace.

tools.name: list_endpoints
tools.description: List all endpoints available in the workspace.

tools.name: list_service_datasources
tools.description: List all service datasources available in the workspace.

tools.name: execute_query
tools.description: Execute a sql query against Tinybird SQL API.

tools.name: server_usage_timeseries
tools.description: Endpoint to get usage timeseries (sessions and users by method) for a specific MCP server over a configurable time window (default 30 days)

tools.name: tool_call_counts
tools.description: Endpoint to get tool call counts and user counts by tool name for a specific MCP server

tools.name: tool_uptime_timeseries
tools.description:
Daily uptime/success rate by tool for a specific MCP server.
    Used for line chart visualization of tool reliability over time.
    Returns daily uptime percentage for each tool_name.
    Uses the same error calculation as method_reliability for consistency:
    rpc_errors = (is_unavailable OR is_server_error) AND is_error_eligible

tools.name: client_usage_timeseries
tools.description:
Daily session counts broken down by client for a specific MCP server.
    Used for stacked bar chart visualization of client usage over time.
    Filters to initialize method only since that's where client_name is set.

servers.name: Todoist
servers.description: Full implementation of Todoist Rest API for MCP server

tools.name: TODOIST_ADD_WORKSPACE
tools.description: Tool to create a new workspace in Todoist. Use when you need a separate workspace to organize projects. Generates UUID and temp_id automatically.

tools.name: TODOIST_CLOSE_TASK
tools.description: This tool marks an existing task as completed in Todoist. It requires the `task_id` of the task to be closed.

tools.name: TODOIST_CREATE_COMMENT
tools.description: Tool to create a new comment in Todoist. Use when you need to add a note to a specific task or project after confirming its ID.

tools.name: TODOIST_CREATE_LABEL
tools.description: Creates a new label.

tools.name: TODOIST_CREATE_PROJECT
tools.description: Creates a new project in Todoist.

tools.name: TODOIST_CREATE_SECTION
tools.description: Tool to create a new section within a specific project. Use when you need to group tasks under a new heading in a project.

tools.name: TODOIST_CREATE_TASK
tools.description: Create a new task in Todoist. This action allows users to create tasks with various parameters including content, due dates, priority, and more. The task can be created in a specific project, section, or as a subtask of another task.

tools.name: TODOIST_DELETE_LABEL
tools.description: Tool to delete a specific label. Use when you need to permanently remove an unused label by its ID after confirming it's not in use. Example: "Delete label with ID 2298391482".

tools.name: TODOIST_DELETE_PROJECT
tools.description: Tool to delete a specific Todoist project. Use when you need to permanently remove a project by its ID.

tools.name: TODOIST_DELETE_SECTION
tools.description: Tool to delete a specific section. Use when you need to permanently remove unused section by its ID after confirming it's not in use. Example: "Delete section with ID 82181370".

tools.name: TODOIST_DELETE_TASK
tools.description: Delete a task from Todoist. This action permanently removes the task and all its subtasks.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_ALL_COMMENTS
tools.description: This tool retrieves all comments associated with a specific task or project in Todoist. It requires either a task_id or a project_id to fetch the comments, and it returns a JSON array of comment objects with details such as id, posted_at, content, and attachment information.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_ALL_PROJECTS
tools.description: Get all projects from a user's Todoist account. This tool retrieves all projects from the authenticated user's Todoist account. The response includes details like project ID, name, color, parent project, sharing status, and other metadata for each project.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_ALL_SECTIONS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve all the sections for a specific project in Todoist. Use when you need to list section structure within a project after selecting the project.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_ALL_TASKS
tools.description: Fetches all INCOMPLETE tasks from Todoist and returns their details. IMPORTANT LIMITATIONS: - This endpoint only returns active (incomplete) tasks - Cannot retrieve completed tasks - use a different endpoint for that - Filter queries using 'completed', '!completed', or 'completed after' will fail - Project references must use existing project names or IDs Common use cases: - Get all tasks: no filter - Get today's tasks: filter="today" - Get overdue tasks: filter="overdue" - Get tasks from specif

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_BACKUPS
tools.description: Tool to list all available backup archives for the user. Use when you need to retrieve and review all existing backups.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_COMMENT
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details of a specific comment by its comment_id. Use when you need full information (content, timestamp, or attachment) about a known comment.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_LABEL
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific label by its ID. Use when you need detailed info about a label after you have its ID.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_PROJECT
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific project by its ID. Use when you have a project ID and need its metadata before display or update.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_SECTION
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific section by its ID. Use when you have a section ID and need its metadata before display or update.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_SPECIAL_BACKUPS
tools.description: Tool to list special backup archives for the user. Use when you need to retrieve all project backups for the authenticated user after creating or managing projects.

tools.name: TODOIST_GET_TASK
tools.description: Tool to retrieve a specific task by its ID. Use when you need to fetch all details of an existing task before processing or display.

tools.name: TODOIST_LIST_ARCHIVED_WORKSPACE_PROJECTS
tools.description: Tool to list all archived projects in a workspace. Use when you need to retrieve archived workspace projects with optional filters or pagination.

tools.name: TODOIST_LIST_FILTERS
tools.description: Tool to list all filters for the authenticated user. Use when you need to retrieve the current set of custom filters.

tools.name: TODOIST_LIST_PENDING_WORKSPACE_INVITATIONS
tools.description: Tool to list pending invitation emails in a workspace. Use when you need to check which email invites are still pending acceptance in a workspace.

tools.name: TODOIST_REOPEN_TASK
tools.description: This tool reopens previously completed task.

tools.name: TODOIST_UPDATE_COMMENT
tools.description: Tool to update a specific comment's content. Use when you need to correct or clarify an existing comment after confirming its ID and the new text.

tools.name: TODOIST_UPDATE_PROJECT
tools.description: Tool to update a specific project's attributes such as name, color, indent, and order. Use when you need to rename or reorder a project after reviewing its current settings. Example: "Update project 2203306141 name to 'Q1 Goals'."

tools.name: TODOIST_UPDATE_SECTION
tools.description: Tool to update a specific section's attributes such as name and order. Use when you need to rename or reorder a section after confirming its ID.

tools.name: TODOIST_UPDATE_TASK
tools.description: Tool to update an existing task's properties. Use when you need to modify a task's details after confirming its ID and new values.

servers.name: Webex
servers.description: A Model Context Protocol (MCP) server that provides AI assistants with comprehensive access to Cisco Webex messaging capabilities.

tools.name: WEBEX_CREATE_ROOM
tools.description: Tool to create a new room. Use when you need to spin up a dedicated space for team collaboration or topic discussion.

tools.name: WEBEX_CREATE_TEAM
tools.description: Tool to create a new team. Use when you need to group rooms under a shared team.

tools.name: WEBEX_GET_TEAM_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific team by teamId. Use when you need full metadata of a team before performing team-related operations.

tools.name: WEBEX_LIST_TEAMS
tools.description: Tool to list teams. Use when you need to retrieve the teams the authenticated user is a member of.

tools.name: WEBEX_LIST_WEBHOOKS
tools.description: Tool to list webhooks. Use after authentication to retrieve webhooks filtered by max or ownership.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_CREATE_MESSAGE
tools.description: Tool to post a message to a Webex room or person. Use when you have a target roomId or private recipient and want to send text, markdown, files, or card attachments.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_CREATE_ROOM
tools.description: Tool to create a new room. Use to create a dedicated space for collaboration after authentication.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_CREATE_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP
tools.description: Tool to add a person to a Webex team by personId or personEmail. Use when granting a user access to a team; requires teamId and one of personId or personEmail.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_DELETE_MEMBERSHIP
tools.description: Tool to delete a Webex membership by its unique identifier. Use when you need to remove a member from a space after confirming the membership exists. Example: "Delete the membership with ID Y2lzY29zcGFjMDczNzA2Njg0ZDliY2YxNDE4NDQyYzQ5NDQzOTExYTk4".

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_DELETE_MESSAGE
tools.description: Tool to delete a Webex message by its unique identifier. Use after confirming the messageId to remove unintended or obsolete messages. Example: "Delete the message with ID Y2lzY29zcGFyazovL21lc3NhZ2UvYWJjMTIzNDU2Nzg5".

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_DELETE_ROOM
tools.description: Tool to delete a Webex room by its ID. Use after confirming the roomId when you need to permanently remove or archive a space. Example: "Delete the room with ID Y2lzY29..."

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_GET_MEMBERSHIP_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific membership. Use when you need metadata for a membership by its ID.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_GET_MESSAGE_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific message. Use when you need full content and metadata by message ID.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_GET_ROOM_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific room. Use when you need full metadata of a specific room before posting messages or updating settings.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_GET_TEAM_MEMBERSHIP_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific team membership. Use when you need metadata for a team membership by its ID.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_LIST_MEMBERSHIPS
tools.description: Tool to list memberships in a Webex room. Use when you need to retrieve or filter membership details by roomId, personId, personEmail, or teamId. Supports limiting the response size.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_LIST_MESSAGES
tools.description: Tool to list messages in a room. Use when you need to retrieve chat history filtered by room, time window, or mentions.

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_LIST_ROOMS
tools.description: Tool to list rooms the authenticated user belongs to. Use after authentication when needing to retrieve spaces filtered by team, type, or sorted. Example: "List my group rooms sorted by last activity."

tools.name: WEBEX_MESSAGING_LIST_TEAM_MEMBERSHIPS
tools.description: Tool to list team memberships. Use when you need to retrieve or filter team membership details by teamId, personId, or personEmail, with optional max limit. Use after confirming the team or person context.

tools.name: WEBEX_PEOPLE_GET_PERSON
tools.description: Tool to get details for a specific person. Use when you have a person's ID and need full profile information. Call after confirming the person's ID.

tools.name: WEBEX_PEOPLE_LIST_PEOPLE
tools.description: Tool to list people in your organization. Use when you need to retrieve people filtered by email, display name, IDs, roles, or location.

tools.name: WEBEX_ROOMS_GET_ROOM_DETAILS
tools.description: Tool to retrieve details for a specific room. Use when you need full metadata of a room before posting messages or updating settings.

tools.name: WEBEX_UPDATE_MEMBERSHIP
tools.description: Tool to update a membership by membershipId. Use when modifying a member's moderator or monitor status in a Webex room.

tools.name: WEBEX_UPDATE_ROOM
tools.description: Tool to update a room's title and settings. Use when you need to rename, lock/unlock, or reassign a Webex space.

tools.name: WEBEX_UPDATE_TEAM
tools.description: Tool to update a team's name by teamId. Use when you need to rename a Webex team. Example: "Change team 12345 name to 'Project X Team'".

tools.name: WEBEX_WEBHOOKS_CREATE_WEBHOOK
tools.description: Tool to create a new webhook for events. Use when you need to register for Webex event notifications.

tools.name: WEBEX_WEBHOOKS_DELETE_WEBHOOK
tools.description: Tool to delete a specific webhook. Use when you need to remove an existing webhook by its ID after confirming the identifier.

tools.name: WEBEX_WEBHOOKS_GET_WEBHOOK
tools.description: Tool to get details for a specific webhook. Use when you need to inspect a webhook's configuration before taking action.

setup_steps.title: Smithery (hosted)
setup_steps.text:
1. Open https://smithery.ai/servers/webex
2. Click Connect and complete OAuth in your MCP client (Claude, Cursor, VS Code, etc.)
3. MCP endpoint: https://webex.run.tools

servers.setup_summary: 1. Open https://smithery.ai/servers/webex

servers.name: Webflow
servers.description: Interact with Webflow APIs to list and edit your site and CMS data.

tools.name: webflow_guide_tool
tools.description: Provides essential guidelines and best practices for effectively using the Webflow tools. Call this tool to understand recommended workflows and important considerations before performing actions. ALWAYS CALL THIS TOOL FIRST BEFORE CALLING ANY OTHER TOOLS.  ALWAYS CALL THIS TOOL FIRST BEFORE CALLING ANY OTHER TOOLS.

tools.name: ask_webflow_ai
tools.description: Ask Webflow AI about anything related to Webflow API.

tools.name: data_cms_tool
tools.description: Data tool - CMS tool to perform actions like get collection list, get collection details, create collection, create collection fields (static/option/reference), update collection field, list collection items, create collection items, update collection items, publish collection items, and delete collection items

tools.name: data_components_tool
tools.description: Data tool - Components tool to perform actions like list components, get component content, update component content, get component properties, and update component properties

tools.name: data_pages_tool
tools.description: Data tool - Pages tool to perform actions like list pages, get page metadata, update page settings, get page content, and update static content

tools.name: data_scripts_tool
tools.description: Data tool - Scripts tool to perform actions like list registered scripts, list applied scripts, add inline site script, and delete all site scripts

tools.name: data_sites_tool
tools.description: Data tool - Sites tool to perform actions like list sites, get site details, and publish sites

tools.name: data_comments_tool
tools.description: Data tool - A comment in Webflow is user feedback attached to a specific element or page inside the Designer, stored as a top-level thread with optional replies. Each comment includes author info, timestamps, content, resolved state, and design-context metadata like page location and breakpoint. Use this tool to inspect feedback discussions across the site and understand where and why they were left.

tools.name: data_enterprise_tool
tools.description: Data tool - Enterprise tool to perform actions like manage 301 redirects, manage robots.txt and more. This tool only works if User's workspace plan is Enterprise or higher, else tool will return an error.

tools.name: asset_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Asset tool to perform actions like create folder, get all assets and folders, update assets and folders

tools.name: get_image_preview
tools.description: Designer Tool - Get image preview from url. this is helpful to get image preview from url. Only supports JPG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, WEBP and AVIF formats.

tools.name: de_component_tool
tools.description: Designer tool - Component tool to perform actions like create component instances, get all components and more.

tools.name: element_builder
tools.description: Designer Tool - Element builder to create element on current active page. only create elements upto max 3 levels deep. divide your elements into smaller elements to create complex structures. recall this tool to create more elements. but max level is upto 3 levels. you can have as many children as you want. but max level is 3 levels.

tools.name: element_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Element tool to perform actions like get all elements, get selected element, select element on current active page. and more

tools.name: element_snapshot_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Element snapshot tool to perform actions like get element snapshot. helpful to get element snapshot for debugging and more and visual feedback.

tools.name: de_page_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Page tool to perform actions like create page, create page folder, get current page, switch page

tools.name: style_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Style tool to perform actions like create style, get all styles, update styles

tools.name: de_learn_more_about_styles
tools.description: Designer tool - Learn more about styles supported by Webflow Designer.Please do not use any other styles which is not supported by Webflow Designer.Please use the long-form alias of a CSS property when managing styles. For example, the property row-gap has a long-form alias of grid-row-gap, margin has long-form alias of margin-top, margin-right, margin-bottom, margin-left, etc.

tools.name: variable_tool
tools.description: Designer Tool - Variable tool to perform actions like create variable, get all variables, update variable
